info.title: Network School OAuth Provider
info.description:
OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect identity provider for Network School.

Lets third-party apps request NS user data via standard OAuth scopes — the same pattern as "Sign in with Google."

## Flows

- **Authorization Code + PKCE** — user-facing "Sign in with Network School" flow
- **Client Credentials** — machine-to-machine, no user context

## Scopes

| Scope | Claims |
|-------|--------|
| `openid` | `sub` |
| `email` | `email`, `email_verified` |
| `profile` | `name`, `picture` |
| `roles` | `roles` (Discord roles, live) |
| `date_joined` | `date_joined` |
| `offline_access` | refresh tokens |

## Authentication

- **User auth**: Discord OAuth2 (NS guild membership required)
- **Sessions**: HS256 JWT in httponly cookie (`ns_session`)
- **Access tokens**: RS256 JWT (auto-generated RSA keys)
- **Client auth**: `client_secret` verified via bcrypt

get.description: Returns all registered OAuth applications, sorted by creation date (newest first). Does not include client secrets.

schema.title: Response List Apps Api Apps  Get

post.description: Creates a new OAuth client application. Returns the `client_id` and `client_secret` — the secret is only returned once at creation time (stored as a bcrypt hash). Scopes are validated against the set of available scopes.

201.description: App created. **`client_secret` is only returned in this response.**

get.description: Returns all apps with status=pending. Requires admin privileges.

schema.title: Response List Pending Apps Api Apps Pending Get

patch.description: Set an app's status to approved or rejected. Requires admin privileges.

get.description: Returns a single OAuth app by its UUID. Does not include the client secret.

patch.description: Partially updates an OAuth app. Only provided fields are changed. Scopes are re-validated if provided.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an OAuth app and its credentials. This cannot be undone.

get.description: Generates a CSRF state token, stores the `next` URL, and redirects the user to Discord's OAuth2 authorization page. After Discord login, the user is sent to `/auth/discord/callback`.

302.description: Redirect to Discord OAuth2 authorization page.

get.description: Handles the Discord OAuth2 callback. Validates state, exchanges the authorization code for tokens, fetches the Discord user profile, verifies NS guild membership (rejects non-members), fetches guild roles, creates/updates the user, sets the `ns_session` cookie, and redirects to the stored `next` URL.

302.description: Redirect to `next` URL with session cookie set.

post.summary: Exchange a session relay code for a session token
post.description: Accepts a single-use code (from the Discord login redirect) and returns a session JWT. The code expires after 60 seconds and can only be used once.

400.description: `invalid_code` — code missing, expired, or already used.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile from the `ns_session` cookie. Used by the consent page to display who is granting access.

post.description: Clears the `ns_session` cookie, ending the user's session.

get.description: Validates the client request and redirects the user to the login page (if unauthenticated) or consent page (if authenticated). The frontend renders login/consent UI, then posts back to `/oauth/authorize/consent`.

302.description: Redirect to frontend `/login` or `/consent` with all authorize params.

get.summary: Get app info for consent screen
get.description: Returns the app name, icon, description, privacy policy URL, and requested scope details. Used by the consent page to render what permissions the user is granting.

post.description: Called by the consent page after the user clicks Allow or Deny. Returns JSON `{"redirect_to": url}` (not a 302) because cross-origin fetch makes the Location header inaccessible. If approved, generates an authorization code and appends it to the redirect URI. If denied, appends `error=access_denied`.

200.description: JSON with `redirect_to` URL containing either `code` + `state` (approved) or `error=access_denied` (denied).

401.description: User not authenticated (no valid session cookie).

post.description:
Token endpoint supporting three grant types:

- **`client_credentials`** — Machine-to-machine. Requires `client_id` + `client_secret`. Returns an access token with no user context.
- **`authorization_code`** — User-facing. Exchanges an authorization code (from `/oauth/authorize/consent`) for an access token. Supports PKCE (`code_verifier`). If `openid` scope was granted, also returns an `id_token`. If `offline_access` scope was granted, also returns a `refresh_token`.
- **`refresh_token`** — Exchange a refresh token for a new access token + refresh token (rotation). Requires `client_id` + `client_secret`.

Client authentication: provide `client_secret` for confidential clients, or omit for public clients (PKCE-only).

400.description: Missing required fields, invalid/expired authorization code, PKCE mismatch, or unsupported grant type.

get.description:
Returns user claims based on the scopes granted to the access token.

Requires a valid Bearer token in the `Authorization` header. The token must have been issued via the `authorization_code` grant (tokens from `client_credentials` have no user context and will be rejected).

**Claims by scope:** `openid` → sub | `email` → email, email_verified | `profile` → name, picture | `roles` → roles | `date_joined` → date_joined.

401.description: Missing Bearer token, invalid/expired token, token has no user context, or user not found.

post.description: Check whether an access token is active. Returns token metadata including `scope`, `client_id`, `user_id`, `exp`, `iat`, `jti`, and `iss`. Returns `{"active": false}` for invalid, expired, or revoked tokens.

200.description: Introspection result with `active` boolean and token metadata.

post.description: Revokes an access token so it can no longer be used. Always returns 200 regardless of whether the token existed.

200.description: Empty JSON `{}`. Token is revoked (or was already invalid).

get.description: Returns all scope definitions including inactive ones.
Returns all active claim names that can be assigned to scopes.

post.summary: Upload a custom icon for a scope

get.description: Returns all claim definitions including inactive ones.
Returns all role names from the NS Discord guild via bot API.
Returns all active OAuth scopes with their descriptions, claims, and icons.

post.description: Upload an icon for an OAuth app. Accepts PNG, JPEG, GIF, and WebP. Max file size: 2 MB. Replaces the existing icon if one exists.

400.description: File type not allowed or file too large.

delete.description: Removes the icon from an OAuth app and deletes the file from storage.

get.description: Returns the public RSA key set used to verify RS256-signed access tokens and ID tokens.
RFC 8414 metadata document. Returns issuer, endpoints, supported grant types, response types, code challenge methods, scopes, and authentication methods.
OIDC discovery document. Returns issuer, endpoints, supported algorithms (`RS256`), scopes, grant types, and authentication methods. Clients can auto-configure from this URL.
Returns `{"status": "ok"}` when the server is running.

status.title: Status
status.description: Approval status: pending, approved, or rejected.
